text:
Once upon a time in a town surrounded by ancient forests, there lived a boy named Arthur. Arthur loved animals—especially owls. Every dusk, he would listen to them calling from their lofty nests.

One autumn, a fierce storm swept through the town. Trees fell like needles, and fierce winds howled. In the shaking ruins of an owl perch, Arthur spotted a young owl he baptized Moonbeam.

Every eveningMoonbeam sang a song about storms. They became kindlinstruments. Arthur learned Owl Speak.

When Spring arrived, Moonbeam flew south.

Years went by.

An expedition came seeking wonder.

They took Moonbeam.

Oh oh.

Arthur flew into anger.

But then Owl Wisdom.

Let them take you wise things.

Moonbeam flew North.

Years grew old.

Arthur lived a quiet life under an old oak.

Once a year, he visited Moonbeam.

Now that’s a story.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
